questions.question: What is the lesson we should always keep in mind?
questions.answer: 'Try Again.'

questions.question: What should you do if at first you don't succeed?
questions.answer: You should try again.

questions.question: What quality should appear if you want to succeed?
questions.answer: Your courage should appear.

questions.question: What will happen if you persevere?
questions.answer: You will conquer and succeed.

questions.question: Should you ever fear failure?
questions.answer: No, you should never fear it; just try again.

questions.question: How many times might you fail before succeeding?
questions.answer: Once or twice, or even more.

questions.question: What should you do if you fail twice?
questions.answer: You should try again.

questions.question: What does the poet mean by 'strive'?
questions.answer: To make a great effort or struggle.

questions.question: Is striving a disgrace?
questions.answer: No, it is no disgrace to strive.

questions.question: What does 'win the race' mean in the poem?
questions.answer: It means to achieve your goal or succeed.

questions.question: What should you do if you don't win the race?
questions.answer: You should try again.

questions.question: What should you do if your task is hard?
questions.answer: Try again.

questions.question: What will 'Time' bring you if you are patient?
questions.answer: Time will bring you your reward.

questions.question: Who else can do the tasks you find difficult?
questions.answer: Other folks (people).

questions.question: Why can other people do it but you can't yet?
questions.answer: Because you haven't used enough patience and effort yet.

questions.question: What is the only thing you should do to succeed like others?
questions.answer: Only keep this rule in view: Try Again.

questions.question: What is the rhyme scheme of the phrase 'Try Again'?
questions.answer: It is used as a refrain at the end of many lines.

questions.question: What does 'heed' mean in the poem?
questions.answer: To pay attention to.

questions.question: Is failure the end of the journey?
questions.answer: No, it is just a reason to try again.

questions.question: Does the poet think we should be ashamed of failing?
questions.answer: No, he thinks we should be brave and continue.

questions.question: What is the tone of the poet?
questions.answer: Encouraging and motivating.

questions.question: What happens to people who give up?
questions.answer: They never reach their reward or succeed.

questions.question: Who is the poem written for?
questions.answer: It is written for anyone facing difficulty, especially children.

questions.question: What is the main value taught in the poem?
questions.answer: Persistence and hard work.

questions.question: How many stanzas are there in this short poem?
questions.answer: Three stanzas.
